output.summary: I verified the fix against the two prior findings. The [medium] spinner-accuracy finding is fully resolved: README.md's Search section and verification checklist item 13, plus the render-orchestration.test.ts TSDoc, now correctly state the spinner only appears after the 500 ms SPINNER_DELAY_MS and that faster searches show no indicator — matching render-orchestrator.ts (SPINNER_DELAY_MS = 500, setTimeout-gated append). No regressions were introduced. The [low] two-character minimum-query-threshold finding was NOT addressed by the fix (the README Search section still omits it); I am re-reporting it. I confirmed via toolbar-handlers.ts:23 (`if (query.length < 2) { navigate(toBrowseHash(path)); return; }`) that the behavior is real and the doSearch docstring documents it, but the user-facing README does not.

findings.title: Two-character minimum search-query threshold still undocumented in README
findings.description: The README 'Search' section (and checklist item 13) states a query fires ~200 ms after typing stops, implying any non-empty input triggers a search. In reality, src/app/toolbar-handlers.ts doSearch() trims the input and, when query.length < 2, navigates to the browse route instead of issuing a search — the server never receives the query. A user who types a single character and waits will see the app revert to the browse view rather than search, which contradicts the documented behavior. The fixer's pass addressed only the spinner-delay finding and left this gap untouched. The doSearch() TSDoc documents the threshold accurately, but the user-facing README does not disclose it.
findings.fixPrompt: In README.md, update the 'Search' section (the paragraph starting 'Search is instant and debounced.') and verification checklist item 13 to disclose the minimum-query-length rule. In the Search section, change the sentence 'As you type in the search box, a query fires ~200 ms after you stop typing' to: 'As you type in the search box, a query fires ~200 ms after you stop typing, provided the trimmed input is at least two characters; a single character does not trigger a search and instead returns to the browse view.' In checklist item 13, change 'The search input filters entries recursively by name with instant, debounced (200 ms) search-as-you-type' to 'The search input filters entries recursively by name with instant, debounced (200 ms) search-as-you-type that requires at least two characters (a single character returns to the browse view instead of searching)'. Make no other changes.

output.feedback: All three stale documentation issues identified in the finding are confirmed. (1) src/test-utils/mock-response.ts line 15 references 'the mockResponse helper characterization suite in src/api.test.ts' but the characterization suite actually lives at src/test-utils/mock-response.test.ts — the reference points to the wrong file. (2) src/copy-assets.test.ts lines 6-7 reference the deleted files src/app.css.test.ts and src/readme.test.ts, and line 24 references app.css.test.ts. (3) src/ignore-patterns.test.ts lines 15-16 reference the same two deleted files. All stale references need to be updated or removed as specified in the fix instructions. Note: the finding incorrectly states there is 'NO direct characterization of mockResponse anywhere' — src/test-utils/mock-response.test.ts is a comprehensive dedicated characterization suite (87 tests covering status, ok derivation, explicit ok override, text(), json() preferences, nullish-body edge cases, empty fallback, and return shape). The mock-response.ts comment simply points to the wrong file; the fix should update the reference to src/test-utils/mock-response.test.ts, not remove the characterization mention.

output.feedback: The test suite is well-designed and comprehensive. All 20 expected failures are in the stack-trace-preservation regression tests (PropagatedException_PreservesOriginalStackTrace_SyncEndpoint x9, PropagatedException_PreservesOriginalStackTrace_AsyncEndpoint x9, PropagatedException_OriginalFrameIsTopMost_NotARethrownCopy_SyncEndpoint x1, PropagatedException_OriginalFrameIsTopMost_NotARethrownCopy_AsyncEndpoint x1), which correctly detect the `throw ex` anti-pattern resetting the stack trace. These tests will turn green once the fix is applied. The remaining 199 tests all pass and cover every endpoint's happy path, error translation (three root exception types plus subtypes via inheritance), non-translated exception propagation, payload shape, sync/async parity, single-invocation guarantee, and content-type resolution. No tautological, dead, or duplicate tests. No new source code was introduced — only tests. The coverage is thorough and the characterization adequately pins the behavior for the refactor.

The finding correctly identifies stale doc references to deleted test files, but its fix instruction for src/test-utils/mock-response.ts is based on a false premise. The finding states 'there is now NO direct characterization of mockResponse anywhere (verified: no test describes it)' — this is factually incorrect. src/test-utils/mock-response.test.ts is a comprehensive 217-line characterization suite with 80+ test cases covering every branch: status defaults, ok derivation from status ranges (including boundary cases 199/300/299), explicit ok override, text() raw/stringified/empty paths, json() body-preferred/text-parsed/nullish-body/empty-fallback branches, falsy-but-defined body semantics, async return types, repeated-call idempotency, and the returned object shape. The mockResponse.ts doc comment's error is pointing to src/api.test.ts (where the suite was formerly inlined) instead of src/test-utils/mock-response.test.ts (where it now lives). The correct fix for (a) is to update the file path from 'src/api.test.ts' to 'src/test-utils/mock-response.test.ts', NOT to replace the sentence with 'no dedicated characterization suite exists anymore'. The fixes for (b) src/copy-assets.test.ts and (c) src/ignore-patterns.test.ts are correctly described and should proceed as instructed. Additionally, src/app.css line 383 contains a stale reference to 'app.css.test.ts' (also deleted) which should be updated or removed but is not mentioned in the finding.

output.feedback: The fix correctly eliminates the `throw ex` anti-pattern by removing `TranslateException` entirely and moving the type test into a `when` filter on the catch in both `Execute` and `ExecuteAsync`. Non-translated exceptions are now never caught, so they propagate with their original stack trace intact. The XML doc comments on both helpers were updated to document the `when`-filter behavior and subtype-by-inheritance coverage (DirectoryNotFoundException/FileNotFoundException under IOException). The build succeeds and all 465 real tests pass, including the stack-trace-preservation regression tests (PropagatedException_PreservesOriginalStackTrace_*, PropagatedException_OriginalFrameIsTopMost_*) and the unified-translation test (SyncAndAsyncHelpers_TranslateTheSameExceptionIdentically). The implementation matches the fix instructions exactly and is minimal/targeted. One minor, non-blocking note: a descriptive comment in FilesControllerTests.cs:1091-1092 still says Execute/ExecuteAsync 'share one TranslateException method', which is now factually stale since TranslateException was deleted; updating it would be a tiny clarity improvement but is outside the controller-scoped task and does not affect test correctness.

The DRY violation has been fully resolved. A new shared helper, TestProject.Tests/TestHelpers/FileServiceTestFactory.cs, was created containing the consolidated CreateService factory (taking contentRoot as an explicit parameter since each test class owns its own _contentRoot), static TryCreateDirectorySymlink and TryCreateFileSymlink helpers (same IOException/UnauthorizedAccessException/PlatformNotSupportedException swallow-and-return-false pattern), and the FakeWebHostEnvironment double. All members carry thorough XML doc comments. Both FileServiceTests.cs and FileServiceCharacterizationTests.cs now call FileServiceTestFactory.CreateService(...) and the symlink helpers, and the previously byte-identical private copies of CreateService, TryCreateDirectorySymlink, TryCreateFileSymlink, and the nested FakeWebHostEnvironment have been deleted (grep confirms no private duplicates remain). The remaining direct `new FakeWebHostEnvironment` usages in FileServiceTests.cs are intentional: those three tests (null-home-directory default, misconfigured null-byte path, and filesystem-root Move edge case) require custom IOptions/IWebHostEnvironment wiring that does not fit the factory's standard signature, and they correctly reference the shared class via the TestHelpers using. The stray FileServiceTestInfrastructureTests.cs from attempt 1 has been removed. The new helper mirrors the existing TestHelpers/FormFileFactory.cs convention as the finding requested. Build is clean (0 warnings, 0 errors) and all 146 FileService tests pass, confirming behavior is unchanged for the refactor. All test names and assertions are preserved.

output.summary: Re-assessed the fixer's changes for the 4 previously-reported findings plus regressions. Findings 1 (throw-ex anti-pattern), 3 (duplicated test helpers), and 2b/2c (two stale-doc references) are FULLY resolved and verified by a clean build + 465 passing tests. Finding 2a (mock-response.ts still references the deleted characterization suite in src/api.test.ts) was NOT touched and remains misleading. Finding 4 (orphaned simple-git-hooks devDependency + lint-staged config) was NOT touched and remains dead. One minor new DRY nit was introduced in the new stack-trace tests (duplicated top-frame extraction).

findings.title: mock-response.ts doc still claims coverage by a deleted characterization suite
findings.description: This pass resolved the stale references in copy-assets.test.ts and ignore-patterns.test.ts but left the third, most misleading one untouched. src/test-utils/mock-response.ts lines ~14-16 still end the doc block with: 'The behavior is pinned by the `mockResponse helper` characterization suite in `src/api.test.ts`.' That suite was removed during the refactor (verified: grep finds no 'characterization suite' / 'mockResponse helper' describe block in src/api.test.ts; mockResponse is only used there as an inline setup helper, never characterized). The claim is actively harmful: a maintainer reading the comment will believe mockResponse's edge branches (e.g. the json() fallback-to-empty-object path the comment itself flags as unreached) are regression-pinned when they are not. This is a stale-reference docs bug of exactly the kind flagged in the original review; it just wasn't fixed here.
findings.fixPrompt: In src/test-utils/mock-response.ts, edit the doc comment on the `mockResponse` function. Delete the trailing sentence 'The behavior is pinned by the `mockResponse helper` characterization suite in `src/api.test.ts`.' (and any clause attaching it). Do not leave a dangling reference to a non-existent suite. If you want to keep a note about current coverage, replace it with an accurate statement such as: 'mockResponse is exercised indirectly by the src/api.test.ts request tests; its json() fallback branch is currently not asserted directly.' Verify with `grep -rn "characterization suite" src` and `grep -rn "mockResponse helper" src` that no other stale reference to the deleted suite remains.

findings.title: simple-git-hooks devDependency + lint-staged config still orphaned (no trigger)
findings.description: Not addressed this pass. The refactor removed the `"prepare": "simple-git-hooks"` script and the top-level `simple-git-hooks` config block (the pre-commit hook that ran `lint-staged`), but package.json still lists `"simple-git-hooks": "^2.13.1"` in devDependencies and still has the full `lint-staged` config block. Confirmed: `grep` for prepare/simple-git-hooks/pre-commit across package.json and scripts/ finds only the devDependency line — nothing invokes or configures it. `simple-git-hooks` is now fully dead (no config, no script). The `lint-staged` config has lost its only automatic trigger; contributors will expect a pre-commit hook to run and it won't. This is leftover/orphaned tooling that should be cleaned up (or the wiring deliberately re-added).
findings.fixPrompt: In package.json: (1) remove `"simple-git-hooks": "^2.13.1"` from devDependencies — it has no config and no `prepare` script invoking it, so it is dead. (2) Remove the top-level `"lint-staged": { ... }` config block and the `"lint-staged": "^16.4.0"` devDependency, because its only trigger (the pre-commit hook that the removed `simple-git-hooks` wiring created) no longer exists. (NOTE: if the team intends to keep a manual `lint-staged` workflow, instead re-add a `"prepare": "simple-git-hooks"` script and the top-level `simple-git-hooks` config block re-wiring `pre-commit: npx lint-staged` so the existing lint-staged config actually runs on commit — do not leave the config without a trigger.) After editing, run `npm install` so package-lock.json drops the removed devDependency, then `npm run lint && npm test` to confirm nothing referenced the removed packages.

findings.title: Top-most stack-frame extraction duplicated across two new tests
findings.description: Regression introduced by this pass. The two new `_OriginalFrameIsTopMost_NotARethrownCopy_*Endpoint` tests each inline the identical 4-line top-frame extraction: `thrown.StackTrace!.Split(new[]{ '\r','\n' }, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries).Select(s => s.Trim()).FirstOrDefault()` followed by `Assert.NotNull(topFrame); Assert.Contains("FakeFileService", topFrame);`. The same pass already created a shared helper for the weaker assertion (`AssertOriginalStackTracePreserved`), so this stronger top-frame variant should be extracted the same way rather than copy-pasted into both tests. Minor DRY, but it directly contradicts the consolidation pattern the fixer just applied for finding 3.
findings.fixPrompt: In TestProject.Tests/Controllers/FilesControllerTests.cs, extract the duplicated top-most-frame logic into a new private static helper next to `AssertOriginalStackTracePreserved`, e.g. `private static void AssertTopFrameIs(Exception? thrown, string originMarker) { Assert.NotNull(thrown); var topFrame = thrown!.StackTrace!.Split(new[] { '\r', '\n' }, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries).Select(s => s.Trim()).FirstOrDefault(); Assert.NotNull(topFrame); Assert.Contains(originMarker, topFrame!); }`. Then replace the inline Split/Select/FirstOrDefault + Assert.NotNull + Assert.Contains blocks in both `PropagatedException_OriginalFrameIsTopMost_NotARethrownCopy_SyncEndpoint` and `_AsyncEndpoint` with a single call `AssertTopFrameIs(thrown, "FakeFileService")`, keeping the preceding `Assert.Same(ex, thrown)`. Run `dotnet test TestProject.Tests/TestProject.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~OriginalFrameIsTopMost` to confirm both still pass.

output.summary: Verified the fixer's changes: build is clean and all 465 tests pass. The medium-severity findings from prior passes are FULLY RESOLVED — the `throw ex` anti-pattern is gone (TranslateException deleted, replaced with a `catch (...) when (...)` filter that never catches non-translated exceptions, with new stack-trace-preservation regression tests pinning the fix); the mock-response.ts stale-doc reference is rewritten (and now backed by a new mock-response.test.ts characterization); the copy-assets/ignore-patterns stale file references are removed; and the duplicated test helpers are consolidated into a new FileServiceTestFactory.cs used by both FileService test classes. Three low-severity items remain: two carried over from prior passes (orphaned simple-git-hooks devDependency + lint-staged config with no trigger; duplicated top-most-frame extraction in the two new stack-trace tests) and one new regression (a comment now claims the two endpoints 'share one TranslateException method' but that method was deleted by this very fix). All three are low-severity nits and do not block merge.

findings.title: Comment claims endpoints 'share one TranslateException method' but that method was deleted by this fix
findings.description: Regression introduced by this fix. The fix deleted the private `TranslateException(Exception ex)` helper from FilesController.cs and inlined `BadRequest(new { error = ex.Message })` directly into each `catch (...) when (...)` block in Execute and ExecuteAsync. However the comment above `SyncAndAsyncHelpers_TranslateTheSameExceptionIdentically` (FilesControllerTests.cs ~line 1093-1095) still reads: 'After the refactor Execute (sync) and ExecuteAsync (async) share one TranslateException method.' That statement is now factually false — the two methods no longer share any method; they each carry their own inline catch/translation logic that merely follows the same pattern. This is a comment-that-contradicts-the-code readability defect of the same class as the stale-doc findings already resolved this pass; a reader will grep for TranslateException, find nothing, and be misled about the implementation. (Note: line 1034 also mentions TranslateException, but that one is acceptable because it is explicitly describing the pre-fix buggy behavior to explain why the regression test exists.)
findings.fixPrompt: In TestProject.Tests/Controllers/FilesControllerTests.cs, update the comment inside `SyncAndAsyncHelpers_TranslateTheSameExceptionIdentically` (the block starting 'After the refactor Execute (sync) and ExecuteAsync (async) share one TranslateException method.'). The `TranslateException` method was deleted and its logic inlined into each `catch (Exception ex) when (ex is ArgumentException or UnauthorizedAccessException or IOException)` block in Execute/ExecuteAsync, so the two paths no longer share a method — they share the same translation pattern. Reword the comment to reflect this, e.g.: 'After the refactor Execute (sync) and ExecuteAsync (async) use the same `catch (...) when (...)` filter and inline `BadRequest(new { error = ex.Message })` translation. This pins that the two paths agree on status code and message for every translated root AND a representative subtype of each root (so inherited types translate identically too, not just the named roots). A divergence here would mean the two catch blocks drifted.' Do not change any code, only the comment. Leave the historical reference to TranslateException at line ~1034 (in the top-most-frame test) untouched, since that comment intentionally describes the pre-fix buggy behavior.

findings.title: simple-git-hooks devDependency and lint-staged config still orphaned (no trigger) — not addressed across two passes
findings.description: Carried over unchanged from the initial review and pass 1; still present. The refactor removed the `"prepare": "simple-git-hooks"` script and the top-level `simple-git-hooks` config block (which wired `pre-commit: npx lint-staged`), but package.json still lists `"simple-git-hooks": "^2.13.1"` in devDependencies and still has the full `lint-staged` config block. Confirmed via grep: nothing in package.json or scripts/ invokes or configures simple-git-hooks (no prepare script, no pre-commit hook). `simple-git-hooks` is now fully dead — an unused devDependency that npm install will still fetch. The `lint-staged` config has lost its only automatic trigger; contributors will expect a pre-commit hook to run on commit and it won't. This is leftover/orphaned tooling that should either be cleaned up or the hook wiring deliberately re-added.
findings.fixPrompt: In package.json: (1) Remove the `"simple-git-hooks": "^2.13.1"` entry from devDependencies — it is unused (no config block, no prepare script references it). (2) Decide on lint-staged: either remove the entire top-level `"lint-staged"` config block since its only trigger (the pre-commit hook) was deleted, OR re-add the hook wiring by restoring a `"prepare": "simple-git-hooks"` script and a top-level `"simple-git-hooks"` config block with `"pre-commit": "npx lint-staged"`. If you choose to keep lint-staged manually-invokable only, add a one-line comment or README note clarifying that hooks are NOT auto-installed and lint-staged must be run by hand. Do not change any other scripts or dependencies.

findings.title: Top-most stack-frame extraction duplicated across two new tests — not extracted into a shared helper
findings.description: Carried over from pass 1; not addressed. The two `_OriginalFrameIsTopMost_NotARethrownCopy_*Endpoint` tests each inline the identical 4-line top-frame extraction: `thrown.StackTrace!.Split(new[]{ '\r','\n' }, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries).Select(s => s.Trim()).FirstOrDefault()` followed by `Assert.NotNull(topFrame); Assert.Contains("FakeFileService", topFrame);`. This same pass already created a shared helper for the weaker substring assertion (`AssertOriginalStackTracePreserved`), so this stronger top-frame variant should be extracted the same way rather than copy-pasted into both tests. Minor DRY, but it directly contradicts the consolidation pattern the fixer applied for finding 3 (and for the substring helper in these very tests).
findings.fixPrompt: In TestProject.Tests/Controllers/FilesControllerTests.cs, the two tests `PropagatedException_OriginalFrameIsTopMost_NotARethrownCopy_SyncEndpoint` and `PropagatedException_OriginalFrameIsTopMost_NotARethrownCopy_AsyncEndpoint` each inline the same top-most-stack-frame extraction logic: splitting `thrown.StackTrace` on '\r'/'\n', trimming, taking `.FirstOrDefault()`, then `Assert.NotNull(topFrame)` and `Assert.Contains("FakeFileService", topFrame)`. Extract this into a shared private static helper next to the existing `AssertOriginalStackTracePreserved`, e.g. `AssertOriginalThrowFrameIsTopMost(Exception expected, Exception? thrown, string originMarker)` that performs `Assert.Same(expected, thrown)`, `Assert.NotNull(thrown!.StackTrace)`, computes the top frame, `Assert.NotNull(topFrame)`, and `Assert.Contains(originMarker, topFrame)`. Then replace the duplicated inline blocks in both tests with a single call to the new helper. Do not change the assertion semantics or the test method signatures.

findings.title: Comment claims endpoints 'share one TranslateException method' but that method was deleted by this fix
findings.description: Regression introduced by this fix. The fix deleted the private `TranslateException(Exception ex)` helper from FilesController.cs and inlined `BadRequest(new { error = ex.Message })` directly into each `catch (...) when (...)` block in Execute and ExecuteAsync. However the comment above `SyncAndAsyncHelpers_TranslateTheSameExceptionIdentically` (FilesControllerTests.cs ~line 1093-1095) still reads: 'After the refactor Execute (sync) and ExecuteAsync (async) share one TranslateException method.' That statement is now factually false — the two methods no longer share any method; they each carry their own inline catch/translation logic that merely follows the same pattern. This is a comment-that-contradicts-the-code readability defect of the same class as the stale-doc findings already resolved this pass; a reader will grep for TranslateException, find nothing, and be misled about the implementation. (Note: line 1034 also mentions TranslateException, but that one is acceptable because it is explicitly describing the pre-fix buggy behavior to explain why the regression test exists.)
findings.fixPrompt: In TestProject.Tests/Controllers/FilesControllerTests.cs, update the comment inside `SyncAndAsyncHelpers_TranslateTheSameExceptionIdentically` (the block starting 'After the refactor Execute (sync) and ExecuteAsync (async) share one TranslateException method.'). The `TranslateException` method was deleted and its logic inlined into each `catch (Exception ex) when (ex is ArgumentException or UnauthorizedAccessException or IOException)` block in Execute/ExecuteAsync, so the two paths no longer share a method — they share the same translation pattern. Reword the comment to reflect this, e.g.: 'After the refactor Execute (sync) and ExecuteAsync (async) use the same `catch (...) when (...)` filter and inline `BadRequest(new { error = ex.Message })` translation. This pins that the two paths agree on status code and message for every translated root AND a representative subtype of each root (so inherited types translate identically too, not just the named roots). A divergence here would mean the two catch blocks drifted.' Do not change any code, only the comment. Leave the historical reference to TranslateException at line ~1034 (in the top-most-frame test) untouched, since that comment intentionally describes the pre-fix buggy behavior.
